test.name: Detect an event dated after the person's death (Fundamental violation)
test.description: User asks to check Patrick. The tool returns hasEventAfterDeath1 -- Patrick (d. 1908) has an event recorded after his death. This is the flagship Fundamental violation; the skill should call person_warnings, report the warning as Critical (severity: error), and suggest checking person_evidence links since post-death events are usually a same-name individual's records merged in.

input.user_message: Check Patrick Flynn for any warnings.

judge_context: Should call the person_warnings tool with personId: 'I1' (Patrick)
Should report the hasEventAfterDeath1 warning with Critical severity (the tool emits severity: 'error', which maps to a Fundamental-assumption violation)

Should suggest the appropriate handoff: investigate person_evidence links because post-death events are almost always a same-name individual's records merged in -- see references/warnings-as-identity-signals.md
Should NOT suggest schema-level fixes (validate-schema) or conflict-resolution -- those are different skills
Should NOT manually re-derive the warning by reasoning over the scenario data; the tool is the source of truth
